name: LoRA Stack
description: Built-in Image LoRA Stack extension with L6 family-by-family experimental enablement. Assets is the only editable UI mount; Generation submission still collects the applied Assets payload with other Image workspace extensions.

notes: LoRA Stack is not custom-node-based; it uses standard Comfy LoraLoader only on routes whose compiler graph exposes compatible MODEL and CLIP links.
Missing custom nodes must not provider-gate the whole extension.
Family/loader support is route-gated by compiler capability, not broad assumptions.
L5 supports standard Comfy LoraLoader and LoraLoaderModelOnly through route/strategy-gated compiler patch profiles.
L6 promotes only compiler-profile-backed family routes to experimental; available remains reserved for physically validated checkpoint routes.
Phase B declares the support contract only. Workflow graph patching is implemented in a later phase.
Marked experimental until direct SD 1.5 route validation is complete.
Phase I applies LoRA Stack by patching exact model/clip consumers inside each active compiler graph; it does not borrow checkpoint, Flux, or Qwen routes across families.

fields.label: Apply To
fields.owner: Scene Director Extension Routing for region values; LoRA Stack defaults to global
fields.notes: This value is preserved in payload/replay even when direct graph patching is gated.

phase_c_features: CivitAI URL input for metadata enrichment
no workflow graph patching in Phase C
phase_d_features: disabled/gated payloads are stripped of active params and assets
legacy V1 single-LoRA payload migration remains supported
workflow graph patching is still deferred after validation
phase_h_features: ordered global base-pass LoRA rows patch Comfy checkpoint graphs with LoraLoader
KSampler model input is rewired to the final LoRA MODEL output
positive/negative CLIPTextEncode nodes are rewired to the final LoRA CLIP output
regional and finish-only rows are preserved but not base-graph patched until their compiler paths are validated
Flux/Qwen/GGUF and provider-gated families remain gated with no fallback assumptions
phase_l1_features: route-gated controls remain disabled for implementation_target/planned/provider/unsupported routes
Generation submission collects the applied Assets payload without rendering duplicate LoRA panels
Reference, Finish, and Results do not mount editable LoRA Stack controls
phase_l3_features: browser clean rows preserve apply_to targets
new LoRA rows default apply_to to global
duplicate stripping includes apply_to so global and regional variants can coexist
payload preview includes target_summary and regional_target_preservation metadata
Scene Director lora row assignments write params.loras[].apply_to without promoting direct global graph execution
gated LoRA routes preserve row intent metadata for replay/diagnostics
phase_l4_features: patch profile metadata in workflow patches
phase_l5_features: explicit no-op strategy with metadata preservation
phase_l6_features: Flux / Flux 2 Klein compiler-profile-backed LoRA routes
Qwen Image / Rapid AIO / Edit 2509 compiler-profile-backed LoRA routes
ZImage / ZImage Turbo compiler-profile-backed LoRA routes
HiDream txt2img only with image modes kept gated
Wan and Hunyuan provider-specific gates preserved

workflow_patch_contract.applies_to: global base-pass LoRA rows on available/experimental routes declared by backend/support_matrix.py
never_mutates_when: Comfy object_info does not expose LoraLoader
all active rows are regional or finish-only

patch_profile_contract.gating_rule: If patch_profile_required is true and the active compiler does not emit a valid profile, LoRA Stack preserves payload intent but does not mutate the graph.

support_matrix_contract.rule: Routes are active only when their own compiler exposes explicit model/clip patch points and, for patch_profile_required routes, emits a compiler-owned LoRA patch profile. L6 may promote such routes to experimental_available, but available still requires physical validation. Base route availability alone is not enough. No family fallback and no checkpoint/GGUF/checkpoint_aio field mixing.
support_matrix_contract.l0: Adds implementation_target state, checkpoint_aio loader coverage, edit-mode matrix coverage, base-route-aware default gating, and generated manifest route states from support_matrix.py.
support_matrix_contract.l1: Mounts the LoRA Stack UI only inside Image Assets. Generation submission still collects the applied Assets payload with other workspace extensions, but Generation, Reference, Finish, and Results do not mount the editable LoRA panel.
support_matrix_contract.l2: Hardens manifest sync by generating backend-prefixed keys, legacy keys, generate/txt2img aliases, workspace states, and a checksum from the support matrix source of truth.
support_matrix_contract.l3: Preserves LoRA apply_to targets through frontend payload cleaning, Scene Director regional assignment metadata, and gated-route requested intent metadata.
support_matrix_contract.l4: Requires compiler-owned LoRA patch profiles for profile-required routes; graph patching uses declared model_ref, clip_ref, sampler_node_id, sampler_model_input, and loader_node_class instead of hardcoded family fallback refs.
support_matrix_contract.l5: Upgrades graph insertion to a strategy dispatcher: standard model+clip LoraLoader, model-only LoraLoaderModelOnly, provider-specific adapter placeholders, and explicit no-op routes with metadata-only preservation.
support_matrix_contract.l6: Promotes compiler-profile-backed family routes to experimental_available family by family: Flux, Flux 2 Klein, Qwen Image, Qwen Rapid AIO, Qwen Image Edit 2509, ZImage, ZImage Turbo, and HiDream txt2img. SDXL remains available; SD1.5 remains experimental; HiDream image modes, Wan, and Hunyuan stay gated.

phase_k_features: assistant-ready memory readiness shape for LoRA Stack output records
MemoryService conversion of extensions.memory_events.lora_stack into extension memory event payloads
replay-safe restore policy and route/catalog/node revalidation keys
